title: lobelia burttii subsp. telmaticola
intro: Lobelia burttii subsp. telmaticola, also known as the marsh lobelia, is a plant species of the Lobelia genus native to the mountainous regions of East Africa. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lobelia burttii subsp. telmaticola are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as improved digestion and reduced fever. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application for skin conditions.
